title: SQL Lab 12: Employees Above Department Average
short_description: Return employees whose salary is greater than their department average.
business_context: People analytics often compares individual compensation against department-level benchmarks.
problem_statement: Return employees whose salary is greater than the average salary in their department.
student_task: Write a query returning emp_id, dept_id, and salary.
learning_goals: Compare row-level values to group-level metrics
production_logs: analytics-review: employee-level detail missing from output
review-note: grouped average alone does not identify above-average employees
solution_query:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT emp_id,
         dept_id,
         salary,
         AVG(salary) OVER (PARTITION BY dept_id) AS avg_salary
  FROM employees
)
SELECT emp_id, dept_id, salary
FROM base
WHERE salary > avg_salary;
explanation: The window average keeps employee-level rows while adding the department benchmark, making the final filter straightforward.
common_mistakes: Grouping by dept_id and losing employee rows
Comparing to the global average instead of department average
Using >= when the prompt says greater than
hints: Keep employee-level rows in the output.
A window AVG lets you compare each row to its department average.
